reasoning: VIX at 21.52 rising sharply (+8.41% overnight from ~19.8) signals fear building—a clear bearish indicator. Overnight gap down $1.18 (0.17% of prior close) confirms selling pressure from overnight session. No major macro events today per calendar; FOMC is 2026-03-18 (16 days out). Sector rotation is mixed: financials weak (-0.87% XLF) but tech slight positive (+0.33% XLK). Ribbon remains BULL (fast 685.24 > pivot 685.03 > slow 684.72, spread 52c) but is under pressure from the gap-down open. This is not a no-trade scenario (VIX in MID regime, no critical events), but the combination of rising fear + negative overnight gap creates a bearish macro backdrop favoring put entries over call entries. Bias: bearish. Expect choppy to directionally-down morning until market digests the overnight pressure.

Gap down $1.18 = moderate bearish overnight sentiment; no retest of prior close at 686.16 in overnight session
